description: Calgary occasion calendar — V1 seed data. 200+ target by end of Week 2.

occasions.name: New Year's Day
occasions.calgary_notes: Strong brunch demand on Jan 1. Many businesses closed so dining out spikes.

occasions.name: Valentine's Day
occasions.calgary_notes: Largest reservation-driven dining occasion of Q1 in Calgary. Prix fixe menus common. Book early or lose cover capacity.

occasions.name: Family Day (Alberta)
occasions.calgary_notes: Alberta-specific long weekend. Family dining out is strong. Brunch heavy. Not well-served by most promotions — families want comfort, not promos.

occasions.name: St. Patrick's Day
occasions.calgary_notes: Bar/pub traffic dominant. Restaurant impact depends heavily on whether you have a bar program.

occasions.name: Easter Sunday
occasions.calgary_notes: Brunch is the dominant occasion. Reservation-based dining. Good Sunday brunch operations see 20–40% above-normal covers.

occasions.name: Good Friday
occasions.calgary_notes: Long weekend start. Lunch and dinner out. Less heavy than Easter Sunday.

occasions.name: Mother's Day
occasions.calgary_notes: Single largest brunch/lunch occasion of the year for Calgary restaurants. Reservation demand is extremely high. Operators who don't activate messaging 2+ weeks out lose cover capacity to competitors.

occasions.name: Victoria Day Long Weekend
occasions.calgary_notes: First major long weekend with good weather. Patio season opens in earnest. Tourism traffic +12% average for Calgary centre restaurants.

occasions.name: Calgary Lilac Festival
occasions.calgary_notes: 17 Ave SW corridor festival. Extremely high foot traffic along the strip. Restaurants with patio or takeout capture significant walk-in business.

occasions.name: Father's Day
occasions.calgary_notes: Strong dinner occasion. Less dominant than Mother's Day but significant. Steakhouses and casual dining see peak utilisation.

occasions.name: Calgary Stampede
occasions.calgary_notes: The single biggest event on the Calgary calendar. 10 days. Massive tourism influx. Restaurants not within the festival zone still see 15–30% above-normal traffic. Stampede breakfast culture is strong — brunch particularly elevated. Competitor promo cadence typically starts 4 weeks out. Western-themed menus and messaging are expected. Authentic local connection is the winning narrative.

occasions.name: Stampede Kick-Off Weekend
occasions.calgary_notes: First weekend of Stampede. Peak of peak. Friday and Saturday evening covers are at annual maximum for most Calgary restaurants.

occasions.name: Canada Day
occasions.calgary_notes: Long weekend. Strong outdoor dining demand when weather cooperates. Patio operators see significant lift.

occasions.name: Heritage Day (Calgary)
occasions.calgary_notes: Alberta Heritage Day long weekend. End of summer feel. Moderate dining out lift.

occasions.name: Calgary Folk Music Festival
occasions.calgary_notes: Prince's Island Park. Draws 15,000+ over the weekend. Nearby restaurants (Kensington, Eau Claire) capture pre/post traffic.

occasions.name: Labour Day Long Weekend
occasions.calgary_notes: Post-summer wind-down. Last big patio weekend. Moderate lift. Some operators see slower traffic as families prepare for school.

occasions.name: Thanksgiving (Canada)
occasions.calgary_notes: Long weekend. Strong family dining occasion — significant share of the Calgary market dines out rather than cooking at home. Sunday Thanksgiving dinner is the peak.

occasions.name: Halloween
occasions.calgary_notes: Bar and lounge traffic is the dominant dining occasion. Restaurant impact is low unless you have a strong bar program or run themed events.

occasions.name: Remembrance Day
occasions.calgary_notes: Statutory holiday. Quieter dining day. Some restaurants close. Not a significant occasion for most operators.

occasions.name: Christmas Season Start
occasions.calgary_notes: Corporate holiday party bookings dominate December. Operators who lock in group bookings by mid-November fill December significantly more efficiently.

occasions.name: Christmas Eve
occasions.calgary_notes: Dinner demand is very high. Many families dine out Christmas Eve. Often busier than Christmas Day itself.

occasions.name: Christmas Day
occasions.calgary_notes: Mixed — many businesses close. Operators who open see strong demand from those not hosting at home. Reservation-only or prix fixe tends to work well.

occasions.name: New Year's Eve
occasions.calgary_notes: Highest demand evening of the year for bar and dining combined. Operators should have prix fixe or special programming confirmed by Dec 1. Cover capacity books out fast.

occasions.name: NHL Playoffs — Calgary Flames
occasions.calgary_notes: If the Flames make the playoffs, bar traffic in Calgary surges significantly on game nights. Restaurants near the Saddledome or with strong TV setups benefit most.

occasions.name: Grey Cup Week
occasions.calgary_notes: CFL championship. Strong bar/pub traffic. Restaurant impact depends on presence of a bar program.

occasions.name: Sled Island Music Festival
occasions.calgary_notes: Multi-venue indie music festival. Strong Beltline and downtown traffic. Late-night dining and bar business is the primary beneficiary.

occasions.name: Calgary International Film Festival
occasions.calgary_notes: 10-day film festival. Moderate dining lift in the Eau Claire and inner-city areas. More relevant for evening dining.

occasions.name: Back to School — Calgary
occasions.calgary_notes: Negative signal for some operators — family dining frequency drops as school schedules tighten. Lunch traffic can shift. Coffee shop/cafe demand increases with student traffic.

occasions.name: Tax Deadline (Canada)
occasions.calgary_notes: Minor negative signal — consumer spending slightly constrained in the week before tax deadline. Not a major factor for most operators.

occasions.name: Patio Season Opens
occasions.calgary_notes: Calgary patio culture is strong — operators with outdoor seating see significant traffic shifts when weather cooperates in May. The first sustained warm week typically triggers a step-change in patio covers.

occasions.name: Calgary Marathon
occasions.calgary_notes: Large running event. Strong brunch demand post-race in the Eau Claire and 4th Street corridor. Hydration-focused menu items see uptick.

occasions.name: Stampede Parade Day
occasions.calgary_notes: Friday parade day. Downtown and adjacent neighbourhoods are flooded. Walk-in traffic peak of the year for many operators. Staff up.

occasions.name: University of Calgary Fall Semester Start
occasions.calgary_notes: Student population returns. Relevant for operators near campuses or frequented by students. Budget-conscious demographic.

occasions.name: Bell Let's Talk Day
occasions.calgary_notes: Mental health awareness day. Some brand alignment opportunity for cafes/community-focused operators. Low commercial demand signal.

occasions.name: Restaurant Week Calgary
occasions.calgary_notes: Annual dining promotion event. Participating restaurants set prix fixe menus. Non-participation is worth analysing — competitor participation can shift market attention significantly.

occasions.name: TELUS Spark Science Centre Events
occasions.calgary_notes: Adult night and special event series. Late-night bar/restaurant traffic in the area when events are running.
